questions.text: The order of a matrix with 3 rows and 4 columns is:
questions.explanation: The order of a matrix is written as rows × columns. So a matrix with 3 rows and 4 columns has order 3 × 4.
Matrix multiplication is NOT commutative. In general, AB ≠ BA. It is associative and distributive though.

questions.text: A matrix is symmetric if A = ________.
questions.explanation: A square matrix A is symmetric if it equals its transpose, i.e., A = A'. This means a_ij = a_ji for all i,j.

questions.text: Find the transpose of a 2×3 matrix.
questions.correctAnswer: The transpose will be a 3×2 matrix with rows and columns interchanged.
questions.explanation: If A is 2×3 matrix, then A' (transpose) is 3×2 matrix obtained by interchanging rows and columns.
